comments.title: Cows Don't Know Better, and What to Do When You Don't Know Either?
comments.content:
Good Evening,

I hope everyone's having a great week!

To Share: I've shared some brief thoughts on how cows don't know what's good for them (okay, and maybe what we can learn from that): https://jpowellrussell.com/#cows_don_t_know_what_s_best_for_them

To Ask: Besides journaling and trying to help others, any particular advice on what to do if you find that might be full of yourself and would like to fix that?

As always, thanks very much to JMG and the commenters here.

To any who will have them, I put forth my blessings and best wishes,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: Cows Don't Know Better, and What to Do When You Don't Know Either?
replies.content: I find that meditation helps that. It's not a fast process, but daily meditation gradually reminds you of the difference between what you actually know (not much) and what you don't know (everything else) -- and that clarifies a very great deal.

replies.title: Re: Cows Don't Know Better, and What to Do When You Don't Know Either?
replies.content:
If you are feeling full of yourself, I recommend these lines from Sir Richard Burton; soldier, writer, explorer and, possibly, Sufi:

'The world is old,

And thou art young;

The world is large,

And thou art small:

Cease, atom of a moment's span,

To think thyself an All-in-All!'

(The Kasidah)

Another thing to bear in mind is that humility is a tool, to enable one to see and be more, which is a legitimate aspiration - even a necessity - for even so humble and transient a creature as Man......

replies.title: Re: Cows Don't Know Better, and What to Do When You Don't Know Either?
replies.content:
When I am full of myself, normally it's relieved by a visit to the nearest outhouse. That's stated mostly in jest, but in truth, I find it humbling to think on (or even meditate on) my basic bodily functions. No matter if I'm writing a novel, running a country, or repairing a tool, I still stop multiple times a day to eat and defecate; and if those processes ever malfunction, all my accomplishments and aspirations are quickly forgotten.

To extend that, it has also humbled me on multiple occasions to do labor or craftwork, particularly to do it with someone far more skilled. I've done many white and blue collar jobs and no matter how learned I get, no matter how clear my perception, I still take hours to get up a few sheets of simple drywall, and I still bumble through a shop manual for a couple hours to fix a car that my more skilled friends can diagnose with an ear and fix within minutes.

I also note that in US culture, money has found a way to equate itself with power and the culture has assigned prestige to those with business success and wealth (even modest wealth.) Not sure where you fall on that spectrum, but the more immersed I am in the US economy the more likely I am to align with those values (becoming more or less full of myself in direct correlation to my business success and wealth.) The antidote for me is to spend time in the garden or the woods; or with farmers, skilled tradesmen, and craftspeople and if the time and work aren't humbling enough (rare) then I ask myself what I have to offer them if the dollar collapses. Over time as my family has expanded, my means have become more modest, and my skills and crafts have improved, I'm finding clearer perspectives and more peace in my levels of pride and humility.

replies.title: Re: Cows Don't Know Better, and What to Do When You Don't Know Either?
replies.content:
Thanks very much for this, that all strikes me as making quite a bit of sense. The focus on craft is one that I "know", but need frequent reminding of. Back to the workbench it is!

And that last point about getting enmeshed in the "money=value" thinking of American culture is well-taken. Considering I teach at a business school, it's *very* likely those values are rubbing off on me more than I might like or realize.

comments.title: Solar Plexus
comments.content:
Hello Mr. Greer,

I had often been directed to place attention on my solar plexus, but didn’t actually know its exact location until I read your books. I know you stress its importance.

My question: this evening I was meditating, first on my breath, but then turned to my solar plexus. I realized that I was able to sense my (what I believe to be) astral body, while focusing on the solar plexus. Is there a reason for this connection, that you know of?
What other advice do you have for developing sensation of the astral body?

Thanks, yet again,

E. S.

replies.title: Re: Solar Plexus
replies.content: The solar plexus is an important center in the astral body as well as the material body, and concentrating on the solar plexus is one of the techniques some schools use to teach people to perceive their astral bodies. It's a good practice to continue; more generally, daily meditation opens up your inner senses in a safe and balanced way, and gives you the capacity to perceive your subtler bodies -- it's not quick, but it works.

replies.title: Re: Solar Plexus
replies.content:
Thank you for this, JMG,

Is there occult school work that links the solar plexus with sensing and working with the Astral Light?

And have you written material relating to such, or do you know of material that explains working with the Astral Light?

replies.title: Re: Solar Plexus
replies.content: Well, since all magic, all clairvoyance, and all divination can be understood (and was so understood by Eliphas Lévi) as working with the astral light, you've got your choice of options! Solar plexus work is rarely a standalone activity; it's something that's incorporated in broader systems of training. I've done this in my book The Way of the Golden Section and, in more detail, in my forthcoming The Secret of the Five Rites.

replies.content:

replies.title: Re: Solar Plexus
replies.content: Depends on what you mean by those labels "adept" and "magus," of course. They're used in many different senses by different authors.

comments.title: Window of Opportunity
comments.content:
Hi JMG,

Thank you for maintaining this invaluable space. And thanks for offering the MOE material. It's pretty transformative. The Blessing Walk alone is worth the free price of entry.

My current status: I've had both the Apprentice and Healer attunements. I'm reviewing all the material from the beginning before taking up the Master practices. I'm managing regular practice of almost everything up to that point, and things are starting to connect more (the theory articles especially, which seemed a bit abstract at first). Except the following that I could use pointers on:

1. In the last few weeks the wheels fell off my discursive meditation practice due to some disruption to routine. I have good momentum with the Druidry Handbook DM approach: a long relaxation period, stable posture and breathing, fair discipline in retracing thoughts back to the theme.

Before restarting I wondered about ways to incorporate the "Window of Opportunity" practice since it's mentioned in the DH. But the MOE practice appears to be a whole other discipline that's an end in itself, rather than a suitable preparation for DM (though it sounds pretty cool tbh). Any tips, beyond the brief instruction in DH to "pay attention", on how this practice can be developed from an AODA/discursive meditation POV? The DH text suggests there's higher levels. I'm conscious it could be something I need to leave for another time but wondered what the options were.

2. Regret to say that I've completely neglected the Kinesiology after making my first attempt. I'm not too sure how to make the best use of it is the issue. Can it, or would you recommend that it be used for general divination about anything at all?

I'm eager to get cracking with the Tree of Life exercises next but it's clear there's no prizes for rushing.

Thanks,

JH

replies.title: Re: Window of Opportunity
replies.content:
1) They're two different systems of meditation and don't really blend well. I recommend doing one or the other for your daily practice.

2) You can certainly use it for general divination. That said, some people find it congenial and others don't. Give it a few more tries, and if it doesn't work for you, don't worry about it.

replies.title: Re: Window of Opportunity
replies.content:
Hi JMG

Sorry I posted my thanks below a bit hastily. If you don't mind, I am curious about its inclusion in the DH (I studied those instructions pretty closely back in the day, to get the basic DM technique). Was it just an inclusion you thought better of later?

Thanks
JH

replies.title: Re: Window of Opportunity
replies.content: I wrote The Druidry Handbook in 2004-3005, while I was still very new to John Gilbert's teachings and hadn't yet had the chance to sort everything out. (That's why it doesn't include the Sphere of Protection, for example.) If I were rewriting it now I wouldn't include that -- but that's water over the bridge at this point.

replies.title: Re: Window of Opportunity
replies.content: 2. For what it's worth, I've taken to asking via kinesiology "is [question] the kind of question this oracle is well-suited to answering?" before I ask a new kind of question that way, and it seems to be working out pretty well. Oh, and also "am I in a state to get good answers from this?" (for example, it seems that one beer is enough to render finger-testing no longer accurate for me).

replies.title: Re: Window of Opportunity

Regards

replies.title: Re: Window of Opportunity
replies.content: Hopefully a helpful data point - I've found kinesiology helpful with specific, defined, mundane questions (a place where my other divination tools are less helpful.) So, I'll ask questions like, "Will I regret eating one bowl of this food right now" when eating something with an ingredient I'm slightly intolerant to, or "will it harm me to have two glasses of this wine over the next two hours" when considering whether to imbibe at a social function, or "will my current, unpleasant symptoms of mucous and cough be reduced by treating myself for viral infection" (and follow that with other possibilities like bacterial infection or allergic reaction.)

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
Good morning JMG,

I have a question about magic and visualization. I really struggle with using my imagination and formulating mind pictures, and I'd like to improve so that I get better results with my magical practice. I'm currently working through your CGD system. The results of my practice thus far have been mediocre, at best.

I found a pretty good chapter on "Visualization and the Training of a Magician" in David Conway's _Magic: An Occult Primer_, but the instructions aren't very detailed. He basically says to start by making mental reproductions of two-dimensional objects, then three-dimensional objects, and eventually work your way to "project[ing] your consciousness to different parts of the room" so you can get a better view of things.

I've been starting with trying to visualize a circle or a square drawn in black, on a white background, but I realize that I really don't understand the specifics of what I'm trying to do.

For example, am I trying to "see" things by projecting them on the back of my eyelids, or are the eyes not involved at all? I'm guessing not since I notice when I tell myself to imagine, say, a cow, I can very briefly see a cow in my mind without involving my eyes in any way - it's just in my mind somewhere. But I can't hold the image for any length of time, much less look at it from all angles like Conway says I should practice doing. If I'm trying to use this other part of my mind and not my eyes, how can I get better at being able to hold the image longer than a quick flash?

I can do a little better when imagining the elemental scenes when doing the LRP or SOP. In that case, I can sort of imagine that the landscape is right outside my body and would be there if I opened my eyes (I can even sometimes feel the heat of the desert on my right and the cold mountain landscape on my left). That's the best I've been able to do.

Also, is the goal to eventually be able to see an imagined image in front of me, with my actual eyes?

Any ideas and/or resources you can suggest for improving would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Okay, this is a common problem. No, you're not going to see things in front of you with your actual eyes. You're seeing these things in what people in an older generation called "the mind's eye" -- it's the same sort of imagery you get when you remember something visual, or when you daydream. The difference is that magical training teaches you to do that at will, and to do things with the imagery.

Most people, when they start, can't hold onto images and can't do things with them. This is normal. Your mission, if you should choose to accept it, is to work on concentration and visualization until you can do these things and do them well. That'll probably take years, by the way.

In the meantime, "sort of imagining" is enough, and it's always helpful to include other senses besides sight, so feeling the heat and the cold is a good plan.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Thank you so much for the clarification. I definitely choose to accept this mission and will keep practicing every day. During meditation this morning, I tried to visualize holding and smelling a cut lemon. I got so close to being able to smell it! I even found my mouth watering a bit. I'll keep at it :-)

I hope you're doing well and appreciate you taking the time to respond to all of us here in this space.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: You may be one of the people who has an easier time using senses other than sight. I'm in the same situation -- it's much easier for me to "feel" something in imagination, as though I was touching it, than to "see" it. If you find that you can get better results with smelling or what have you, be sure to include that whenever you're visualizing things -- it really does add to the experience, and the effectiveness.

And I can imagine holding a lemon in my hands better than I can see it. I'll be sure to practice developing these other senses as well as visualizing. Thanks again!

comments.content:
Dear JMG,

I asked last week about the feeling function and the OSA, thanks again for your replies to my questions.

I recently went through the OSA (also the later grades) over the past year and a half or so. It was very useful, but my readings of Jung, von Franz, and James Hillman have shown me the issue of working with my superior functions (intuition, thinking) at the expense of my inferior functions. They have also shown the need for me to work on integrating my anima.

I was going to start the Dolmen Arch, but I thought I could the following instead beforehand:

- Spend a couple of months meditating on the Eros and Psyche tale. I'd use blue color breathing. I read the instructions in the DA about meditating through the Mabinogion in the first grade, and I would apply that technique to the Eros and Psyche meditations.

- I'm mostly through a re-read of the Golden Ass, and I have ordered Marie-Louise von Franz's essays about the Golden Ass, which should include Eros and Psyche.

One problem I see with this is that I now react in an OSA way to events in daily life. As we discussed last week, that is based in the thinking function. If I want to train my inferior feeling function, then that involves actually feeling the emotions, however clumsily, and keeping my superior thinking function out of the way. So I'm unsure if I need to undo what I learned from the OSA.

The other problem is that I've used my thinking function to make a plan for developing my feeling function! All the Jungian writers I listed warn of the dangers of this.

replies.title: Re: baffling phone ads
replies.content:
Smartphones, home phones, computers with microphones can all listen to conversations etc.

If one wants to avoid said situations one can:

-remove battery from smartphone if at all possible (practically impossible by now except for older models), no juice no transmission of radio waves.
-use a portable faraday cage i.e. envelope or holder or wrap made out of metallic mesh that will block out reception and sending of radio waves. Airplane mode can be circumvented.
-use multiple layers of tape or another material to block computers mic or disconnect if connected via usb cable or what not.
-unplug landline from wall socket or disconnect wire on phone.
-use a stainless steel or other metallic container that as it's own cover, a heavy saucepan and it's lid would be very good, and store your phone that is off, I mean has been shut down, in it, you do not want to fry your phone, this will prevent the radio waves from getting to the phone. You can also shutdown the phone and wrap it in layers of aluminum foil.

The most important thing to remember is that any one of the recent smartphones can be activated remotely to spy on you, even when it is off, but if the radio waves cannot carry the signal or reach the phone nobody can snoop on what is going on. Yeah and the adage of no juice no go is very appropriate for stopping any electric or electronic device.

Landline phones have to be unplugged to be 100 % safe, or when plugged blocking the microphone/s can reduce the risk, will muddle the sound but the latest versions of software can reconstruct the original conversation, up to a point.

Same applies to other electronics: they must not be able to communicate with the internet so if off and unplugged from the power source (computers) or the battery removed (laptops), and unplugged from the internet literally unplugged, disconnected, and the router does not have wifi, or it can be disabled via a switch that can be trusted to not be bypassed (has to be mechanical), then you are good, and as usual no juice (router) no go.

All electronic off buttons on electronic and electrical devices can be circumvented, but that is not the case with what is called a "real switch", as an example your classic standard (not a smart switch) light switch to your room, once off nobody from a distance can switch it on, the contacts do not touch each other and cannot touch each other without being pushed together, all else is...

And remember that all that can be listened to can also be viewed via video camera on phone, computer, tablet...

No juice (electricity or power) no go...that is the surest way to privacy.

Welcome to our modern world's reality!

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
1. Discursive meditation revealed something I did not know about myself. I realized I have problem with imagination. What I always thought is imagination is actually visualization of something others imagined. I never had any problems with THAT. However, when left to my own devices I tend to think in very abstract terms and stop at that. Coming up with different particular examples for an abstract concept is always a struggle. I try to remember things that I read or encountered in life, but without more explicit prompts I’m usually pretty lost. I think it is a lack of imagination on my part.

I read a lot and that helps somewhat. However, I’m not happy with the fact that examples, analogies and metaphors do not come for me as easy as I’d like and most of the time they are not even mine. Or maybe it should be this way and THAT is the point of discursive meditation?
I’m not sure what can I do to improve that.

2. I recently realized that what I thought is metaphor is actually analogy. Analogies are easier for me than metaphors because I’m used to “translating” words into numbers and graphs and back. Coming up with words that translate into images and feeling is a different story. And sometimes I don’t even recognize s metaphor for what it is.
Same question as before: what could be done to develop metaphorical thinking.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: In both cases, the only thing I know that will do the job is practice. Any time you come up with an abstraction you want to understand better, fumble your way to a particular example. You can use an example from someone else to start with, but then change it in at least one way, preferably more than one. That's how the imagination develops -- it's always a matter of modifying ideas already encountered. Any time you encounter a metaphor, similarly, think about it, and come up with three other metaphors that more or less express the same things.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
2. I have found that getting into poetry has been forcing me to come up with metaphors, and not only because "that's how you're supposed to do it" - if you're following some kind of traditional form, you *have* to work out ways to squeeze your ideas into the space available, and often metaphors end up being a good way to do that.

Also, Ron Hale-Evans talks about a way to purposely generate kennings (a term from Old Norse poetry for the metaphors used in Eddic and Skaldic poetry) in the book Mind Performance Hacks, but also, helpfully, in this here essay: https://kennexions.ludism.org/old/kenning.html

It still takes work, but at least there's some structure!

Hope these help,
Jeff

This is an excellent question, but also an incredibly broad one. The short version is that diet affects almost everything, and energy healing is an unusually sensitive part of everything.

As with JMG, I wouldn't presume to second guess your teacher. Talking to them about any concerns you have would be a good idea though.

There are some systems of spirituality that interact poorly with the sort of pollutants that come from factory farmed meat. In a Vajrayana context; I have actually seen vegetarianism recommended when the technically correct position (as per the same religious tradition), was that factory farmed meat, particularly in large quantities, were what was off limits. In such a tradition, your current diet would actually be acceptable despite the tendency to advise a vegeterian diet.

The ayurvedic tradition is quite broad, so it isn't really clear which technique you will be using or what school of practice you are associated with. Keeping that in mind, some Ayurvedic techniques rely on subjecting the patient to a hefty dose of Sattva in order to balance out the Doshas. Meat, especially red, is considered Rajic and thus interferes with the ability to properly manipulate the sattva-guna. In this context, adding meat to your diet (even ethically sourced and moderately consumed) will make it significantly harder to perform the technique correctly.

If you are chatting up an ayurvedic guru and a druid, it sounds like you are looking at some cool stuff. I hope this has been of use to you, and wish you luck in your path.

comments.title: Planetary charity gone wrong
comments.content:
A couple of weeks ago I asked for advice on planetary charity to help with a quiz I'd entered into, and where I stood a chance of winning some money. Mercury was indicated, so I duly made my vow on Wednesday, ruled by Mercury, on the hour of Mercury, and proceeded over the next relevant number of days to make a daily donation to a relevant charity on the relevant planetary hour. I also devoted that hour to making an offer of frankincense, and played Kimberly's arrangement of the Orphic hymn to Mercury/Hermes. Some days I added a few more devotional offerings/meditations/recitations. The day itself was also very auspicious. Not only was it a Wednesday, but I was up during the planetary hour too. I also invoked good luck, and banished bad luck, during my daily SOP.

Now, I know I'm breaking the magical rule of silence sharing all this, but my reason is because despite all the above, which I thought was pretty apt and on point, it went wrong. Spectacularly wrong. Instead of my tongue being loosened and my mind quickened, everything went blank, and it was as if my ability to reason and speak coherently was taken away. It was exactly the opposite of what I asked for, and to make matters worse, my ineptitude will shortly be broadcast nationally. But that's not all.

On my way back home, I was very nearly involved in a motorway pile up which would have been fatal if I had not escaped it by the breadth of a cat's whisker. So far from winning anything, the day cost me money, and I'm still in shock after that near death experience. I get the feeling that I was given a divine eff you, to be frank. (And no, Mercury is not afflicted in my chart, in fact, the opposite.)

Now, perhaps this little anecdote of mine will only serve as a data point. But it leaves me with various questions, first of which is whether I should take this as a sign to abandon magic altogether? It was such a spectacular fail. Am I being warned off? I tried to reason that perhaps my escaping death in that accident is proof of luck, for instance, and that the working succeeded in that respect, but it smacks too much of the kind of reasoning you get from the quaxxinated, where they sit in hospital sick as a dog but thank their lucky stars they got quaxxed because 'otherwise it surely would have been so much worse'. No. This was from start to finish extraordinarily bad luck. There's no way I can rationalise any of it.

Any advice, anyone? JMG how often do you think does this sort of failure happen? Could you perhaps venture a guess as to what a realistic expectation of success could be? I've been veering between the extreme of thinking it's just a case of 'some you win, some you lose' and 'get the hell out and stay out, this isn't for you' in my meditations, so I'm thoroughly confused. I'm just so disappointed, this was my first major effort at any magical working, and what worries me, I guess, is that if I get things wrong like I did here, the raspberry jam can hit others in the vicinity - as it did indeed do in this case, I wasn't the only one affected both in the competition and later in the motorway incident. So you can see why this frightened me. Are there people who should not take up magical practice in this life?

Hmm. If I was a semi-conscious aggregate of people's quiz-show related attitudes, I might be kind of mad about people trying to do something that those people would interpret as cheating, especially if it was an American quiz show that was hooked into American mass-culture identities about fairness and meritocracy. (The bearers of which identities currently consider themselves kind of embattled.)

There are a lot of people involved in the experience of a game show, and not just the audience, and it is an extremely controlled ceremonial environment with a large amount of emotion invested into the idea of the rules being kept, in a way even more so even than a courtroom, and the whole game show industry is plausibly somewhat hooked together and could be involved in a sort of mutual defense pact.

When I saw your original proposal, it seemed at the time like a strange thing to not have misgivings about. Maybe next time the next time I see a proposal like that I'll say something?

Was your petition for winning, or just for focus and memory? I would be more surprised at the latter petition having that outcome. Focus and memory are more compatible with the idea of a meritocratic test, since that's something you could keep doing in the rest of your life when you're doing the work your aptitude for which was being tested. Myself, I might have petitioned for a globally efficient allocation of prizes or viewer attention or whatever. (That gets into the question of whether it's right to participate in quiz shows at all, and which ones might be better or worse, which is hard for me to consider fairly because Martial and Solar virtues -- showing off, competition, being an exemplar -- are somewhat in my shadow.)

I think JMG has a recommendation for doing a divination before any nontrivial magical operation. I forget if you mentioned checking with one of those at the time. If you don't have something like that that you trust, you can't check with it to see whether you should keep going, but that wouldn't be an extra cost since in that case presumably you shouldn't keep going anyway because you wouldn't be able to have it warn you off of anything.

Please don’t be so quick to give up magic. I’ve been practicing from a young age and I’ve had workings fail, I’ve had them blow up in my face and I’ve also had them do pretty much nothing. I can’t say why this working didn’t work for you and I won’t pretend to know, but in cases where I’ve had things blow up in my face it was because I was doing something I shouldn’t have been doing. This happened when I was much younger and my magical ethics were a touch loosey goosey but it doesn’t sound like that’s the case here. I do divination before embarking on bigger workings and sometimes I’m told explicitly not to do things for reasons that aren’t always clear to me at the time and I suspect if I did do those workings despite divination telling me not to they would also blow up in my face even if they’re sound to do ethically speaking. Again, I don’t know your situation and I’m not trying to insinuate you’ve done anything unethical, this is just from my own experience.

As some further data, in cases where nothing has really come from my workings at all, it was either a case of self sabotage or situations where my working was too broad and things weren’t focused enough.

Despite some failures, which have been very important learning experiences for me, I’ve had more success than not over the years. So please don’t give up. I’m not JMG but I would encourage you to cut yourself some slack. You just went through some serious and stressful stuff. It may be a while - possibly a long while - before you understand what went wrong here. Divination may not be useful to you here until you’ve calmed down, but journaling and prayer might be really helpful. If you have a friend or diviner you know and trust you could ask them to do a reading to see if there’s any insight there. These are just suggestions, please feel free to take or leave any of them. I wish you all the best success in the future.

As I see it, tree spirits are not trees. They're the guardian spirits of trees and groves, and they are in fact on a different line of spiritual evolution. So, yes, a tree spirit might well evolve into a forest spirit or eventually into a divine being.

Trees themselves, though they're further along than other plants, are much simpler in structure than even relatively simple animals. comments.title: Parasites
comments.content:
Hello John and All!

my question today has to do with the nature of the class of organisms we call parasites. To your knowledge what might be the spiritual basis for these creatures existing? that is, what would a soul benefit or gain from existing as a parasite?

I ask because today I found myself diagnosed with scabies, a little mite parasite that burrows in one’s skin, and does little more than cause itchiness (other than being contagious). As I reflect upon this life experience and attempt to heal/purge myself from this parasite I think it could be beneficial to attempt a greater understanding of where parasites fall in the greater scheme of things.

Thank you very much for your time and space, and for sharing your knowledge.

Best wishes, J

replies.title: Re: Parasites
replies.content: All living things are parasites; that is to say, all living things feed on other living things. Cows are parasitic on grass, sharks are parasitic on fish, human beings are parasitic on all kinds of other living things -- we're broad-spectrum parasites, capable of feeding on just about anything alive. Plants are parasitic on the soil. The creatures that get called parasites are simply that class of living things that parasitize on things much larger than they are. Those mites are feeding on you the way you feed on -- well, whatever you had for dinner last night; you object to it the same way moose object to being fed on by wolves. And moose respond to wolves by trying to disembowel them with their hooves, so you're not being especially out of line in trying to kill the mites.  It's a rough world out there.

replies.title: Re: Small Dolmen Arch question.
replies.content:
Here's the text of the Calling of Daear:

This Calling follows the same pattern as the others. Turn to the north. With the first two fingers of your right hand trace the symbol of earth. In the invoking mode it is traced starting from the place where the circle meets the line and going around the circle clockwise, then drawing the line downward. Visualize it in green flame, and then fill it with paler, transparent green flame. Then say: “By the green gate of the tall stones and the great bear who guards the starry heavens, and in the great name ELEN, I invoke the earth, its gods, its spirits, and its powers. May I receive the blessings of earth this day and always, that I may attain Gwynfydd in this life. May my body be healed by the ways of Nature.”

Elen is the goddess of dawn and springtime, as well as the mistress of the old straight trackways, older than the Romans, that still puzzle wayfarers in the British countryside. She rules the dragon current flowing along the old tracks, and thus is a powerful symbol of life and strength within the earth. Her name is pronounced “ELL-enn.” If you prefer to work with Irish deities, Boann, the Irish cow goddess, is a good choice for earth; her name is pronounced “BO-unn.” Christian Druids usually invoke St. Uriel the archangel in this quarter; pronounce the name “OO-ree-ell.”

When you finish the invocation, pause for a time, and concentrate on earth. Imagine the land in which you live, with whatever vegetation flourishes there growing on it. Be sure to bring in the sounds and scents of the landscape. As you picture this, feel the powers and blessings of earth flowing into you through your solar plexus. Imagine your body filled with the green light of earth, and feel yourself becoming so solid and firm that you could resist the mightiest force in the universe.

Then say, “I thank the earth for its gifts,” and trace the symbol in its banishing mode, starting from where the circle joins the line, tracing the circle counterclockwise, and then draw the line downwards. Say the following: “And with the help of the powers of earth, I banish from within me and around me all unbalanced manifestations of earth. I banish them far away from me.” Concentrate on the idea that all unbalanced factors in your life are swallowed up by the earth and buried far beneath soil and stone. Take as much time as you need on this visualization.

For the next two or three weeks, include this section in your daily practice: perform the Elemental Cross, then invoke and banish by air, then invoke and banish by fire, then invoke and banish by water, then invoke and banish by earth, then perform the Circulation of Light. Practice it until you have this phase of the ritual by heart, and can feel the earth respond to the ritual. During the time you spend doing this, pay attention to any of the symbols of the Gate of Earth that show up in other aspects of your life.

comments.title: FHR - Litany of the Tree of Life
comments.content:
I've been doing some comparative study between what is presented in the Litany of the Tree of Life and the material in the Fellowship of the Hermetic Rose in the interest of making a comparable work centered on the FHR. I suppose I'm trying to understand the deeper logic of the systems and I had a few questions. (If these all end up being meditation homework, that's fine too!)

1) The Litany lists the supernal triad as the 4 Aces, the 4 Fours, and the 4 Eights respectively. Is it a reasonable assumption that the FHR tree also has those attributions? I recall the Fool either being associated with Kether or the path between Tiphareth and Kether, so it seems likely there might be some difference in attribution.

2) Would the FHR work with the traditional Godnames and Archangel attributions from sources like the Golden Dawn, Paths of Wisdom and the Office of the Holy Tree of Life? I seem to recall at one point Paths of Wisdom was listed as a good extra curricular for students in the Magickal Order of the Golden Dawn.

3) Do the tarot paths and spheres given in the FHR papers match their Golden Dawn tarot correspondences for the elements, planets, and zodiac? (ie does the Devil correspond with the sign of Capricorn in addition to being the Place of Ignorance?)

replies.title: Re: FHR - Litany of the Tree of Life
replies.content:
1) Yes.

2) Yes.

3) John didn't use the GD astrological attributions to the Paths -- he considered them to be forced and artificial. The symbolism given in the FHR rituals, together with whatever meanings each students worked out for the tarot trumps, were what he used for the Paths.
